label: Funding

seo.title: Funding Solutions Hub | Moonshine Capital
seo.description: Business, real estate, and personal funding paths plus AI matching, calculators, and comparison tools.

hero.headline: Find the Right Capital Before You Apply Blindly Like a Maniac.
hero.subheadline: Business funding, real estate capital, personal funding, and strategy tools organized into one clean command center.

problem: Most buyers do not know which capital lane fits their situation, so they apply blind and waste time.
Different verticals need different proof, different objections, and different language to feel credible.
Generic pages miss the moment when the user is comparing options and ready to act.
solution: A vertical landing page should start with the exact pain, explain the right lane in plain language, and close with one action that matches the buyer's urgency.

proof.title: One page, one buyer story
proof.description: The page is structured around the reader's situation, not a generic product overview.

proof.title: Reusable template
proof.description: The same layout can support many verticals while keeping the copy tailored.

proof.title: Better CTA match
proof.description: Primary and final calls to action can reflect the buyer's readiness level.

howItWorks: Open with the problem the vertical already feels.
Explain the offer with one clear promise and three supporting benefits.
Use proof, use cases, and objections to remove friction before the CTA.

useCases.title: Pre-qualification page
useCases.description: Ideal when the visitor needs to know whether they should apply, prepare, or look elsewhere.

useCases.title: Industry-specific campaign
useCases.description: Ideal for paid traffic or outbound where the audience expects language that sounds native to their niche.

useCases.title: Content hub landing page
useCases.description: Ideal when the page needs to connect educational content to a specific conversion path.

objections.a: Not if the pain points, proof, examples, and CTAs are actually changing with the vertical.

objections.q: How many verticals should I launch first?
objections.a: Start with three to five. Enough to learn, not enough to drown in maintenance.

objections.q: What makes a vertical page worthwhile?
objections.a: It should either improve conversion, improve ranking intent, or make outbound campaigns more believable.

finalCta.label: Generate the Next Vertical
finalCta.description: Use the same structure, but swap in the buyer's language, proof, and objections.
